tags.name: Arrays
tags.description: Contiguous data, scans, and position-based reasoning.

tags.name: Prefix Sums
tags.description: Aggregate ranges and split points with running totals.

tags.name: Implementation
tags.description: Direct simulation with careful case handling.

tags.name: BFS
tags.description: Layered shortest paths on unweighted graphs.

tags.name: Greedy
tags.description: Build a valid optimum through locally best choices.

tags.name: Binary Search
tags.description: Search on answer using a monotonic feasibility check.

problems.title: Bookstore Balance
problems.summary_md: Split a day of sales into two consecutive parts with the smallest possible difference.
problems.testset_note: Covers short arrays, equal splits, and uneven totals.

sample_cases.note: Balanced split appears in the middle of the array.
All sale counts are equal, so every split is symmetric.

problems.title: Delivery Network
problems.summary_md: Find the fewest roads needed to move a package between two depots in an unweighted network.
problems.testset_note: Connected, disconnected, and trivial one-node cases.

sample_cases.note: A shortest path exists and uses two roads.
The destination depot is disconnected from the start.

problems.title: Meeting Room Schedule
problems.summary_md: Accept the largest number of non-overlapping meetings in a single room.
problems.testset_note: Includes ties, chained meetings, and dominant long intervals.

sample_cases.note: Choosing short meetings beats one long overlapping interval.
Every meeting can be accepted in a clean chain.

problems.title: Router Placement
problems.summary_md: Place a fixed number of routers so the minimum distance between any two routers is as large as possible.
problems.testset_note: Varied spacing patterns for monotonic feasibility checks.

sample_cases.note: Classic sample where three routers fit with distance three.
With two routers, the answer is the distance between the farthest houses.
